svcEmpty: No Services match the filter. Registered via
svcGitHint: git source of the service repo (http(s):// / git:// / ssh / file://)
svcGitHintShort: git source of the service repo
svcNameHint: kebab-case: service name in the registry
svcOverviewProse: Scenarios of this service are available via the git repo
svcRefHint: git tag or branch — service version
svcRefreshHint: opt. auto-refresh of the git repo; empty — no auto-refresh
svcIncEmpty: This service has no incarnations yet. Created via scenario
svcScenariosUnavailable: The scenarios catalog is not exposed yet (endpoint
svcScenariosEmpty: No scenarios in the catalog yet.
svcRefsUnavailable2: does not respond or does not exist). Use the current
svcRefsUnavailable3: or specify a ref manually on upgrade.
svcRefsEmpty: The git repo has no tags or branches.
svcDeregisterWarn: will be removed from the registry. The git repo
svcDeregisterWarn2: is left untouched — the service can be registered again. Incarnations of this service remain but lose their registry link.
svcErrGitPattern: Expected a git URL: http(s):// / git:// / ssh / file://
svcErrRefRequired: Specify a git ref (tag or branch)
svcErrRefreshFormat: Duration in the format 5m / 1h / 30s; empty — no auto-refresh
svcSchemaNotDeclared: State structure is not declared (service.yml::state_schema: is empty).
svcSchemaUnavailable: State-schema unavailable: endpoint returned {{status}} (old Keeper or repo unreachable).
pluginSigilDisabledBody: The plugin allow-list (Sigil) is not active in this cluster. Sigil is enabled on the Keeper side — ask the Keeper administrator to enable SigilSvc in the configuration.
pluginCrumbs: Sigil allow-list of plugins (Keeper computes sha256 itself from the host's local cache)
pluginEmpty: No Sigil grants match the filter. Granted via
pluginNotFound2: in the registry. Grants are issued via
pluginSha256Prose: Keeper computed the hash itself from the host's local cache. Integrity authority is sha256 + Keeper's signature (not the git tag ref).
pluginAuditEmpty2: events for this record found in the journal.
pluginKindsProse: A plugin's namespace defines how Keeper runs it:
pluginKindModSummary: Destiny steps (Apply on Soul). Also relay beacon plugins (pull-monitor for Vigil checks).
pluginKindCloudSummary: CreateVM / DestroyVM. Invoked keeper-side via core.cloud.provisioned.
pluginKindSshSummary: SSH key source for keeper.push (agentless push delivery).
pluginRegisterTitle: Allow plugin into the Sigil allow-list
pluginRegisterCrumbs: Keeper reads the binary + manifest from the host's local cache by (namespace, name) (single-slot), computes sha256 itself and signs the Sigil block. The client does NOT send the hash or signature. ref is an operator-asserted version label (NOT git-verified).
pluginNamespaceHint: mod — soul_module / soul_beacon, cloud — cloud_driver, ssh — ssh_provider
pluginErrTagRef: single path segment (v1.2.3); a branch ref with a slash will be rejected with 422
pluginErrNotFound: Plugin not found in the host's cache. Deploy the binary by namespace/name first; ref is an operator-asserted label.
pluginErrConflict: An active grant for (namespace, name, ref) already exists — revoke first.
rbacPermissionsHint: May be left empty — add later via Role permissions.
rbacEditBuiltinWarn: This is a builtin role — the server will return 409 role-builtin. Editing is disabled.
rbacEditReplaceProse: Replace semantics: the checked set fully replaces the existing one. Tick the permissions you need in the catalog below.
rbacPermCatalogEmpty: Permission catalog is unavailable. The role's existing permissions are kept on save.
rbacPermPreserved: Permissions outside the catalog (kept as is):
rbacDeleteWarn: Role permissions and membership are cascade-deleted. The action is irreversible.
rbacDeleteBuiltinWarn: This is a builtin role — the server will return 409 role-builtin. Deletion is forbidden.
rbacDeleteNoOperators: The role has no operators — deletion affects only the record itself.
rbacAssignProse: The operator will get all permissions of the selected role. The operation is idempotent.
rbacAssignAllRoles: The operator is already a member of all cluster roles.
auditEmpty: No audit events match the filter.
loginHint: There is no /v1/auth/login endpoint yet, so paste the JWT manually: the bootstrap token from the keeper init --archon file, or a token issued by POST /v1/operators/{{aid}}/issue-token. The token is verified by pinging GET /v1/incarnations?limit=1.
loginErrTokenFormat: does not look like a JWT (three base64url segments separated by dots expected)
loginErrNetwork: Could not reach Keeper. Make sure the Operator API is available.
helpOpenapiLead: Keeper serves an interactive API viewer (Stoplight Elements) at /docs. The OpenAPI 3.1 spec is JWT-protected — use the viewer instead.
helpOpenapiJwtHint: The viewer will prompt you to paste an Archon JWT to authorise requests.
helpMcpLead: Model Context Protocol listener for integration with Claude Desktop and other MCP clients. Transport stdio over HTTP/SSE, see keeper.yml listen.mcp.addr.
helpMcpHint: For Claude Desktop, set the endpoint in claude_desktop_config.json under the key mcpServers.soul-stack.
helpDocsLead: Documentation and ADRs live in the soul-stack/docs/ repository.
provPolicyDesc: Controls which methods can be used to create new operators (Archons). Does not affect login for existing operators.
provPolicyDefaultHint: No policy set — all methods are allowed (default behaviour).
provPolicyAntiLockout: The list cannot be empty — this would block creation of new operators.
